--- WINDOW  2026/07/16 21:29:24  [Code.exe] ● Please help me turn off the anoying BT c • Untitled-3 - vaccancies-fetcher [WSL: Ubuntu] - Visual Studio Code
----------------------------------------------------------------------------

--- WINDOW  2026/07/16 21:29:25  [Code.exe] diagnostics.sh - vaccancies-fetcher [WSL: Ubuntu] - Visual Studio Code
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
  [LMB]
  [Ctrl]
  [Clipboard]
  #!/usr/bin/env bash
  
  ROOT="${1:-squashfs-root}"
  
  if [[ ! -d "$ROOT" ]]; then
      echo "Usage: $0 <squashfs-root>"
      exit 1
  fi
  
  green='\033[1;32m'
  yellow='\033[1;33m'
  red='\033[1;31m'
  reset='\033[0m'
  
  ok()   { echo -e "${green}[+]${reset} $*"; }
  warn() { echo -e "${yellow}[!]${reset} $*"; }
  bad()  { echo -e "${red}[-]${reset} $*"; }
  
  echo "==== Firmware inspection ===="
  echo
  
  ########################################
  echo "== build.prop =="
  
  if [[ -f "$ROOT/build.prop" ]]; then
      grep -E \
          'ro.build.tags|ro.board.platform|ro.product.device|ro.product.model|ro.build.version.release|ro.build.display.id' \
          "$ROOT/build.prop"
  
      grep -q 'test-keys' "$ROOT/build.prop" && ok "Built with test-keys"
  else
      bad "build.prop not found"
  fi
  
  echo
  
  ########################################
  echo "== SSH =="
  
  if [[ -f "$ROOT/usr/local/sbin/sshd" || -f "$ROOT/usr/bin/sshd" ]]; then
      ok "OpenSSH server present"
  else
      bad "No sshd binary"
  fi
  
  CFG="$ROOT/usr/local/etc/sshd_config"
  
  if [[ -f "$CFG" ]]; then
      grep -E \
          'PermitRootLogin|PermitEmptyPasswords|PasswordAuthentication|AuthorizedKeysFile|HostKey' \
          "$CFG"
  else
      warn "No sshd_config"
  fi
  
  echo
  
  ########################################
  echo "== passwd =="
  
  if [[ -f "$ROOT/etc/passwd" ]]; then
      grep '^root:' "$ROOT/etc/passwd"
      grep '^sshd:' "$ROOT/etc/passwd"
  
      if grep -q '^root::' "$ROOT/etc/passwd"; then
          ok "Root has empty password field"
      fi
  fi
  
  echo
  
  ########################################
  echo "== rc5 =="
  
  if [[ -f "$ROOT/etc/init.d/rc5" ]]; then
      grep -nE 'ssh|telnet' "$ROOT/etc/init.d/rc5"
  fi
  
  echo
  
  ########################################
  echo "== inetd =="
  
  if [[ -f "$ROOT/etc/inetd.conf" ]]; then
      cat "$ROOT/etc/inetd.conf"
  fi
  
  echo
  
  ########################################
  echo "== OTA certificates =="
  
  if [[ -f "$ROOT/etc/security/otacerts.zip" ]]; then
      unzip -l "$ROOT/etc/security/otacerts.zip"
  fi
  
  echo
  
  ########################################
  echo "== Interesting binaries =="
  
  for f in \
      usr/local/bin/bdpprog \
      usr/local/sbin/sshd \
      usr/bin/sshd \
      usr/sbin/telnetd \
      etc/init.d/upg_prog
  do
      if [[ -e "$ROOT/$f" ]]; then
          ok "$f"
      fi
  done
  
  echo
  
  ########################################
  echo "== bdpprog strings =="
  
  if [[ -f "$ROOT/usr/local/bin/bdpprog" ]]; then
      strings "$ROOT/usr/local/bin/bdpprog" \
          | grep -Ei 'ssh|telnet|debug|factory|engineer|diag|shell|root|login|dropbear' \
          | sort -u
  fi
  
  echo
  
  ########################################
  echo "== update binary =="
  
  if [[ -f "$ROOT/etc/init.d/upg_prog" ]]; then
      strings "$ROOT/etc/init.d/upg_prog" \
          | grep -Ei 'rsa|sha|crc|verify|cert|signature|openssl|x509'
  fi
  
  echo
  
  ########################################
  echo "== Executables =="
  
  find "$ROOT"/{bin,sbin,usr/bin,usr/sbin,usr/local/bin,usr/local/sbin} \
      -type f -perm -111 2>/dev/null \
      | sed "s|$ROOT||" \
      | sort
  
  echo
  echo "Done."
  [LMB]
  [LMB]

  ```sh
  #!/usr/bin/env bash
  
  ROOT="${1:-squashfs-root}"
  
  if [[ ! -d "$ROOT" ]]; then
      echo "Usage: $0 <squashfs-root>"
      exit 1
  fi
  
  green='\033[1;32m'
  yellow='\033[1;33m'
  red='\033[1;31m'
  reset='\033[0m'
  
  ok()   { echo -e "${green}[+]${reset} $*"; }
  warn() { echo -e "${yellow}[!]${reset} $*"; }
  bad()  { echo -e "${red}[-]${reset} $*"; }
  
  echo "==== Firmware inspection ===="
  echo
  
  ########################################
  echo "== build.prop =="
  
  if [[ -f "$ROOT/build.prop" ]]; then
      grep -E \
          'ro.build.tags|ro.board.platform|ro.product.device|ro.product.model|ro.build.version.release|ro.build.display.id' \
          "$ROOT/build.prop"
  
      grep -q 'test-keys' "$ROOT/build.prop" && ok "Built with test-keys"
  else
      bad "build.prop not found"
  fi
  
  echo
  
  ########################################
  echo "== SSH =="
  
  if [[ -f "$ROOT/usr/local/sbin/sshd" || -f "$ROOT/usr/bin/sshd" ]]; then
      ok "OpenSSH server present"
  else
      bad "No sshd binary"
  fi
  
  CFG="$ROOT/usr/local/etc/sshd_config"
  
  if [[ -f "$CFG" ]]; then
      grep -E \
          'PermitRootLogin|PermitEmptyPasswords|PasswordAuthentication|AuthorizedKeysFile|HostKey' \
          "$CFG"
  else
      warn "No sshd_config"
  fi
  
  echo
  
  ########################################
  echo "== passwd =="
  
  if [[ -f "$ROOT/etc/passwd" ]]; then
      grep '^root:' "$ROOT/etc/passwd"
      grep '^sshd:' "$ROOT/etc/passwd"
  
      if grep -q '^root::' "$ROOT/etc/passwd"; then
          ok "Root has empty password field"
      fi
  fi
  
  echo
  
  ########################################
  echo "== rc5 =="
  
  if [[ -f "$ROOT/etc/init.d/rc5" ]]; then
      grep -nE 'ssh|telnet' "$ROOT/etc/init.d/rc5"
  fi
  
  echo
  
  ########################################
  echo "== inetd =="
  
  if [[ -f "$ROOT/etc/inetd.conf" ]]; then
      cat "$ROOT/etc/inetd.conf"
  fi
  
  echo
  
  ########################################
  echo "== OTA certificates =="
  
  if [[ -f "$ROOT/etc/security/otacerts.zip" ]]; then
      unzip -l "$ROOT/etc/security/otacerts.zip"
  fi
  
  echo
  
  ########################################
  echo "== Interesting binaries =="
  
  for f in \
      usr/local/bin/bdpprog \
      usr/local/sbin/sshd \
      usr/bin/sshd \
      usr/sbin/telnetd \
      etc/init.d/upg_prog
  do
      if [[ -e "$ROOT/$f" ]]; then
          ok "$f"
      fi
  done
  
  echo
  
  ########################################
  echo "== bdpprog strings =="
  
  if [[ -f "$ROOT/usr/local/bin/bdpprog" ]]; then
      strings "$ROOT/usr/local/bin/bdpprog" \
          | grep -Ei 'ssh|telnet|debug|factory|engineer|diag|shell|root|login|dropbear' \
          | sort -u
  fi
  
  echo
  
  ########################################
  echo "== update binary =="
  
  if [[ -f "$ROOT/etc/init.d/upg_prog" ]]; then
      strings "$ROOT/etc/init.d/upg_prog" \
          | grep -Ei 'rsa|sha|crc|verify|cert|signature|openssl|x509'
  fi
  
  echo
  
  ########################################
  echo "== Executables =="
  
  find "$ROOT"/{bin,sbin,usr/bin,usr/sbin,usr/local/bin,usr/local/sbin} \
      -type f -perm -111 2>/dev/null \
      | sed "s|$ROOT||" \
      | sort
  
  echo
  echo "Done."
  ```
  result in:
  ```txt
  ==== Firmware inspection ====
  
  == build.prop ==
  ro.build.display.id=OPENMASTER.user.mtk07926.20190704.204139
  ro.build.version.release=1.29
  ro.build.tags=test-keys
  ro.product.model=JBL Bar 9.1

  # ro.build.product is obsolete; use ro.product.device
  [+] Built with test-keys

  Next i connected the bar to a router and my computer to it to inspect which ports are open in the running device
  (its running a newer version of the firmare but those caracteristics above dont tend to change from version to version)

  Unknow command
  Command list:
  dd start, startup dmp application
  dd stop, stop dmp application
  dd cd [index1] [id], change to other container -> index1 is dms index index2 is object id 
  dd back, return to upper container
  dd sort [server index] [given prop], get sort cap, prop may null
  dd sc [server index] [given prop], get search cap, prop may null
  dd dev, list current dlna device
  dd obj, list container's object
  dd obj_start_index [start] [cnt], list container's object from index and get cnt objects
  dd obj_detail, list container's object more detail
  dd obj_exist, list the exist objects which not closed in DMP
  dd sleep, sleep dmp thread
  dd wakeup, wakeup dmp thread
  dd session_id [index] [string id], set object session id 
  dd get_1 [index] ([check]), get a file 
  dd get_2 [index], get a file with range (0 ~ size-1) setting
  dd get_3 [index], get a file with range (step 1024k bytes) setting
  dd get_loop [loop_cnt], get all file cnt times
  dd get_ls [index] [start_range] [size], limited random access get 
  dd open [index], open an object
  dd read [size], read an object at given size
  dd close, close an object which opened
  dd seek [on/off], open all seekable
  dd dump [on/off], dump date in http client read seekable
  dd search, search dlna device again

      mw.aud.risc_dec.open [0 ~ %d]
      mw.aud.risc_dec.close [0 ~ %d]
      mw.aud.risc_dec.sdl [level]
         [level]: n=none/e=error, a=api, i=info
  Usage: 
  use pts [inc/dec] [adjust_value_in ms]
      pts inc 100 => APTS increase 100ms
      pts dec 200 => APTS decrease 200ms
  use vol set [ch_name][vol] to start
  use vol get [ch_name] to start
      ch_name: c, l, r, ls, rs, lsr, rsr, sw, all
      vol    : 0 ~ 100
      Ex: vol set all 100
      Ex: vol get rs
  mw.aud.wfd.set [set_type] [set_value]
  1. Set APLL Adjust Level
     mw.aud.wfd.set apll_lvl [level]
     level: 1 ~ 100
  2. Set non-sync duration threshold
     mw.aud.wfd.set dur [time_in_seconds]
     time_in_seconds: >0 (default setting: 15s) 
  3. Set inconvenient non-sync threshold
     mw.aud.wfd.set inconv [time_in_ms]
     time_in_ms: >100 (default setting: 200ms) 
  mw.aud.wfd.apll_thld [threshold_ms]
  threshold_ms: 20 ~ 120
  use [on][ch_cnt][order] to start
      order: Pair0: L/R   ; Pair1: C/LFE ; Pair2:SLB/SRB 
             Pair3: SL/SR ; Pair4:Ch9/Ch10
      Ex: 2ch;output LR : wifiaudio on 2 0x0FFF0
      Ex: 8ch;output x,x,SLB/SRB,SL/SR : wifiaudio on 8 0x32FF
  use [off]  to stop
  use [st]  to show status
  use [pdi] to show delay time
      mw.aud.spdif.outch [ch_num]
      [ch_num]
          0: IEC select Downmix2ch as output
          1: IEC select L/R    as output
          2: IEC select Ls/Rs  as output
          3: IEC select C/LFE  as output
          4 :IEC select Ch7/Ch8  as output (Ex.Lsr/Rsr)
          5 :IEC mute

  {WGL(SETS)} ws_gmd
  Debug mode = 
  {WGL(SETS)} wb_gdl
  {WGL(SETS)} ws_smd [mode]
                [mode]: 
                        m = mgr's debug level is used for all widget components
                        s = individual component's debug level is used.
                        a = the AND result of mgr's and individual component's debug level is used.
                        b = the OR result of mgr's and individual component's debug levels are used.
                        a = individual component's debug level in mgr's debug level is used.
                        b = both mgr's and individual component's debug levels are used.
  wb_gdl

  Set Widget-Sets Basic Functions debug level
